items.title: Fugitive prisoner with alleged links to bikie gang arrested in rural Darwin

children.content: Northern Territory police officers have arrested Isaac Thompson, 26, in Darwin River, more than 48 hours after he escaped custody during a medical transfer.

image.altText: White man with dark brown hair, brown eyes, thick brown eyebrows with a moustache and beard.

items.title: NT minister claiming travel allowance when staying at property she owns

children.content: It comes after Jo Hersey's CLP colleague criticised Labor politician Manuel Brown for claiming the same allowance at times when staying at his Palmerston home.

image.altText: A female politician sitting down and smiling, inside a parliamentary chamber.

items.title: A bus, a solar-powered stage and a journey across remote Australia

children.content: The Guts tour is a roaming, solar-powered convoy that snakes its way through some of the most remote communities in the Northern Territory, bringing together bands from the city and the bush.

articles.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

image.altText: podcast image NAIDOC week

articles.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

items.title: Single mother of six hit with $6k power bill after months of no communication

children.content: A single mother of six young children is paying off a $6,000 power bill after the Northern Territory's main energy retailer failed to send her a bill for almost 12 months. She's among thousands of Jacana Energy customers facing significant bills.

image.altText: A woman with straight brown shoulder length hair in a black tshirt, sitting at her kitchen table reading her bills.

items.title: Man, 34, in custody after girl, 17, found dead in remote NT home

children.content: Northern Territory Police say the girl was found inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, and was declared dead at the scene. A 34-year-old man known to the woman has been arrested.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Galiwin'ku on Elcho Island.

items.title: Remote NT MLA admits to claiming travel allowance during stays at city home

children.content: Manuel Brown's dumping from the role comes after weeks of scrutiny over his driving record and confirmation he allowed two of his underage children to drive his personal car on an outstation.

items.title: Alice Springs tourism operators celebrate funding after toughest days

children.content: After several challenging years for tourism operators in central Australia, there is hope an incentive of free flights for visitors and a new grant fund will supercharge tourism in the region.

image.altText: A man and a woman standing together in warm clothing

items.title: Online bookmaker Betr shifts operations from NT to Tasmania

children.content: With sports bookmaker Betr granted a licence to operate in Tasmania, independent MP Andrew Wilkie slams the government for "rolling out the red carpet" despite the state admitting its regulations are not up to scratch.

image.altText: Gambling app on mobile phone screen.

items.title: World Cup quarterfinals are upon us. Here's what you need to know

children.content: The World Cup has whittled 48 teams down to just eight, with the quarterfinals upon us and the finish line in sight. Find out all you need to know about these monumental matches.

articles.title: Quick hits: Belgium trolls Trump as Egypt fires up over 'fixed' World Cup

children.content: An all-time blow-up from Egypt's coach headlines the day at the World Cup, as the fallout from Belgium's win over the US continues. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: A collation of photos of men in soccer jerseys.

articles.title: 'Perversion of justice': Politicians launch bid to investigate FIFA boss

children.content: A group of European politicians launches a bid to begin an investigation into how and why a red card for an American football star was overturned at the World Cup.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino in blue suits peering over the golden FIFA Club World Cup trophy on the Oval Office desk

articles.title: 'We spoke for Palestine': Egypt fans on historic World Cup after heartbreaking loss

children.content: After initial disappointment after a late loss to Argentina, many Egypt football fans are joyously reflecting on the team's historic campaign, while using the stage to show support for Palestinians.

items.title: What time is the State of Origin Game III decider tonight?

items.title: The faces in the crowd at the Ayatollah's funeral

children.content: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men they see as central to it.

items.title: Bondi survivor says YouTube's 'false flag' video decision 'inexplicable'

items.title: Critical drugs could be pulled from PBS amid pricing stoush

children.content: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

items.title: Qld business fined $1,200 after sparking million-dollar fire ant scare

children.content: The unnamed business shipped a pallet of second-hand bricks infested with fire ants to mine sites in the Bowen Basin in mid-2025, sparking four rounds of aerial baiting at a cost of $1 million.

items.title: Prince Harry loses privacy case against Daily Mail, calls ruling 'whitewash'

items.title: Netanyahu warns Trump against F-35 deal with Türkiye
